title: Console script entry point is not exercised by the CLI tests
reasoning: The semantic feature under review is the installed `mint` console command, but the integration helper always invokes the package with `python -m mint_cli`, and the direct smoke test calls `cli.main()` in-process. Those paths validate parser/handler behavior but would not catch packaging or entry-point drift where the published `mint` executable no longer resolves to `mint_cli.cli:main`.
recommendation: Add a packaging smoke test that invokes the installed `mint` command, at minimum for `--version` or `--help`, in the same environment used for release validation.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests exercise module execution and direct function calls, but no included test launches an executable named `mint`.
suggestedRegressionTest: Install the project into an isolated environment or use the built wheel, then run `mint --version` and assert a zero exit code plus the expected version string.
minimumFixScope: One CLI packaging smoke test that executes the console script rather than `python -m mint_cli`.

history.reasoning: The original evidence still exists: tests/test_cli.py:16-34 and tests/e2e/test_cli_e2e.py:16-29 still use python -m mint_cli, tests/test_cli.py:219-251 still calls cli.main() directly, and src/mint_cli/cli.py:263-275 still defines main. The gap is now covered by tests/test_cli.py:149-162, which runs Path(sys.executable).parent / "mint" with --version and asserts the installed console-script output. pyproject.toml:21-22 still declares mint = "mint_cli.cli:main", and .github/workflows/offline-ci.yml:21-26 installs the package before running mint doctor and pytest. A local targeted pytest run failed only because this uninstalled Homebrew Python environment has no /opt/homebrew/opt/python@3.13/bin/mint, which confirms the test targets the real installed executable rather than python -m.
commands: rg -n "def run_mint|test_cli_main_direct|def main|console_scripts|project\.scripts|mint_cli\.cli|python -m mint_cli|\bmint --(version|help)" tests/test_cli.py src/mint_cli/cli.py pyproject.toml setup.cfg setup.py
rg -n "subprocess|python -m mint_cli|\bmint --(version|help)|console_scripts|project\.scripts|entry_points|mint_cli\.cli|run_mint|test_.*cli|test_.*mint" tests src pyproject.toml setup.cfg setup.py
